title: disperis nemorosa
intro: Disperis nemorosa, also known as the woodland disperis or woodland pygmy iris, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Disperis nemorosa include its ability to treat various ailments such as wounds, ulcers, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. Disperis nemorosa can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat specific health conditions.
